title: Move Semantics 5
description: Understanding the rules for mutable and immutable references is crucial. You cannot have a mutable reference while immutable references exist.
instructions: Fix the code by ensuring mutable and immutable references don't overlap.
hints: You cannot have two mutable references to the same value at the same time.
Finish using one mutable reference before creating another.
Reorder the operations so that y is done being used before z is created.
explanation: Rust's borrowing rules prevent having two mutable references to the same data simultaneously, which prevents data races at compile time. By finishing the use of `y` before creating `z`, each mutable borrow's scope doesn't overlap with the other. The compiler tracks when references are last used (Non-Lexical Lifetimes) to determine if borrows overlap.
